title: Upon Peason. Epig.
content:
Long locks of late our zealot Peason wears,
    Not for to hide his high and mighty ears;
    No, but because he would not have it seen
    That stubble stands where once large ears have been.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Upon Peason. Epig."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
